# The Apothecary — LifeApothecary

> Science-backed DIY replacements for everyday commercial products. Every recipe is grounded in real chemistry, cites actual mechanisms of action, and honestly rates its effectiveness against the commercial product it replaces.

## What This Site Offers

- 35 ingredient profiles with full chemistry, mechanisms of action, safety data, sourcing, and regulatory status
- 26 DIY recipes spanning oral care, skin, hair, cleaning, laundry, kitchen, health, and garden
- Honest effectiveness scores (1-10 vs commercial) with transparent reasoning about where DIY falls short
- Structured JSON metadata for every ingredient and recipe

## Content Structure

### Recipe Pages
Each recipe at /recipes/{slug}/ includes:
- What commercial product it replaces and cost comparison
- Complete ingredient list with links to ingredient profiles
- Step-by-step method with chemistry explanations
- Effectiveness score vs commercial equivalent (1-10 with reasoning)
- Safety warnings and limitations
- Full markdown content with citations

### Ingredient Pages
Each ingredient at /ingredients/{slug}/ includes:
- Chemical properties (formula, CAS number, molecular weight, pH, solubility)
- Mechanism of action at the molecular level
- Safety data, warnings, and contraindications
- Cost and sourcing information
- Regulatory status (FDA, EU, TGA)
- Cross-references to recipes that use this ingredient

## Categories

- Oral Care: toothpaste, mouthwash, whitening, oil pulling
- Skin & Body: moisturizer, deodorant, lip balm, sunscreen
- Hair: shampoo, conditioner, scalp treatment
- Cleaning: surface cleaners, glass, degreaser, descaler
- Laundry: detergent, softener, stain removal, whitening
- Kitchen & Food: broth, fermentation, bread, spices
- Health: supplements, saline, herbal medicine, bath soaks
- Garden & Home: pest control, fertilizer, wood stain

## Key Principles

- Not "natural is always better" — some commercial products are genuinely superior, and this site says so
- Every claim links to a mechanism; if evidence is weak, that is stated explicitly
- Not a substitute for medical advice
- All effectiveness scores include transparent reasoning about limitations

## Useful URLs

- Homepage: https://ncwardell.github.io/LifeApothecary/
- Sitemap: https://ncwardell.github.io/LifeApothecary/sitemap.xml
- Recipe JSON data: https://ncwardell.github.io/LifeApothecary/Recipes/json/{slug}.json
- Ingredient JSON data: https://ncwardell.github.io/LifeApothecary/Ingredients/json/{slug}.json

## Example Recipes

- Remineralizing Clay Toothpaste (8/10 vs commercial, ~$0.03/use)
- Natural Deodorant (8/10 vs commercial, ~$0.05/use)
- Complete Laundry System (7/10 vs commercial, ~$0.08/load)
- All-Purpose Cleaning System (9/10 vs commercial, ~$0.02/use)
- Whipped Tallow Balm (9/10 vs commercial, ~$0.10/use)

## Example Ingredients

- Baking Soda (NaHCO3) — acid neutralizer, gentle abrasive, deodorizer
- Bentonite Clay — mineral-rich montmorillonite for detox and remineralization
- Coconut Oil — lauric acid-rich carrier with antimicrobial properties
- White Vinegar — 5% acetic acid for cleaning, descaling, and preservation
- Sodium Percarbonate — oxygen bleach (Na2CO3 + H2O2) for laundry and stain removal
